title: pyrus coronaria var. dasycalyx
intro: Pyrus coronaria var. dasycalyx is a lesser-known variety of the Pyrus coronaria species, commonly known as the cornelian cherry or European cornel. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Central and Eastern Europe, and is valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
